title: PM must take responsibility for foreign policy failure and resign: J&K Congress chief
content:
The J&K Congress chief has called for the Prime Minister's resignation.
*   The demand is based on alleged failures in the country's foreign policy.
*   The Congress leader insists the PM must take responsibility for these failures.

title: Rajasthan Assembly passes Bill to scrap two-child norm for panchayat elections
content:
Here's the summary in 3 bullet points:

*   The Rajasthan Assembly has passed a Bill.
*   This Bill aims to scrap the existing "two-child norm."
*   The norm previously barred individuals with more than two children from contesting panchayat (local body) elections.

title: Prime Minister to launch projects worth ₹10,800 crore at Kochi event
content:
The Prime Minister is scheduled to launch new projects.
*   These projects have a combined value of ₹10,800 crore.
*   The launch event will take place in Kochi.

title: SRMIST, Anabond enter into technology transfer agreement for next-gen solar cell encapsulators
content:
SRMIST and Anabond have entered into a technology transfer agreement.
*   The collaboration focuses on developing and implementing next-generation solar cell encapsulators.
*   This partnership aims to bridge research innovations with industrial application for advanced solar energy solutions.

title: Mobilisation for a column on denotified tribes in Census forms picks up pace
content:
Here's the summary in 3 bullet points:

*   Momentum is building for the inclusion of Denotified Tribes (DNTs) in upcoming Census forms.
*   The primary demand is for a dedicated column in the Census specifically for DNTs.
*   Organized efforts to achieve this recognition are intensifying.

title: If BNS distinguishes between throwing and administering acid, RPwD Act must too: Supreme Court
content:
The Supreme Court has ruled that the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (RPwD) Act must differentiate between throwing acid and administering it.
*   This directive is based on the new Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) already making such a distinction in its legal framework.
*   The ruling aims to ensure consistency in legal treatment and provisions for victims, depending on the specific nature of the acid attack.

title: Government sends feelers to Opposition on women’s quota law implementation
content:
The Government is reaching out to the Opposition.
*   The purpose is to discuss the implementation of the women's quota law.
*   This outreach is an exploratory step to gauge their support or cooperation.

title: Professors promoted as Principals in government arts and science colleges
content:
Professors have been promoted to the role of Principals.
*   These appointments are specifically within government arts and science colleges.
*   This signifies a change in the administrative leadership of these public educational institutions.

title: Medical negligence: HC asks govt. to provide free medical assistance, education to child
content:
Here's the summary in 3 bullet points:

*   The High Court (HC) has intervened in a case involving medical negligence.
*   The HC has directed the government to provide specific support to the child affected by the negligence.
*   This support includes both free medical assistance and free education for the child.

title: India will not raise petrol prices despite global crude headwinds: Government sources
content:
India will not increase domestic petrol prices.
*   This decision stands despite challenging global crude oil conditions (headwinds).
*   The information has been confirmed by government sources.
